student_answer:
1. A Docker image is a read-only template that contains the application code, runtime, 
libraries, and dependencies needed to run software. A Docker container is a running 
instance of that image. The image is like a blueprint, while the container is the live 
executable environment created from it. Multiple containers can be started from the same 
image. 
 
2. Docker Compose helps us deﬁne and run multiple related services together using one 
YAML ﬁle. In development, this is useful when an app depends on services like a database, 
Redis, or backend API. It makes startup, conﬁguration, and networking easier and more 
consistent. 
 
3. Containerization is useful in a web application project where the frontend, backend, and 
database must run consistently on every developer machine. Docker avoids “it works on 
my machine” problems by packaging the same environment for everyone.

1. A Docker image is the same as a container, but image is the installed one and container 
is another name for it. 
 
2. Docker Compose is mainly used to make Docker faster and improve internet connection 
between containers. 
 
3. Containerization is useful because it makes code run more quickly than normal 
programs.

student_answer:
1. A Docker image is used to create a container. The image contains the software and 
setup, and the container is the running version of it. The image does not run by itself, but 
the container does. 
 
2. Docker Compose is used when we have many containers and want to run them together. 
It is helpful for development because it saves time and makes commands simpler. 
 
3. It is useful when building a project with a backend and database, because both can run 
inside containers instead of installing everything directly on the computer.
